title: Memoized List Rendering
description:
Optimize a product list where every item re-renders on every keystroke in an unrelated search input.

### Requirements

- Wrap the `ProductItem` component with `React.memo` so it only re-renders when its own props change
- The search input filters products. Typing in the input should NOT cause non-matching items to re-render
- Each `ProductItem` receives a `renderCount` ref that increments on every render — display it for debugging
- Add a render counter at the top showing total renders across all items
- Ensure that items still update correctly when their data actually changes (e.g., toggling a favorite)

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Search input | `search` |
| Product item | `product-{id}` |
| Render count (per item) | `render-count-{id}` |
| Total renders | `total-renders` |
| Favorite button | `fav-{id}` |
constraints:
- Must use React.memo with a proper comparison
- ProductItem must only re-render when its own data changes
- Filtering must still work correctly
- The total render counter must accurately track renders
hints: React.memo performs a shallow comparison of props by default. Make sure you're not passing new object references on each render.
Use useCallback for the onToggleFavorite handler to maintain reference equality.
The render count ref should use useRef — increment it inside the component body (not in an effect).
